definitions.text: Having a value of; proper to be exchanged for.
examples: How much / What is your house worth? - Now it's worth half what I paid for it. So it'd sure would be worthwhile to repair before putting it for sale.
Cleanliness is a virtue worth more than others.
This rickety beater of a car isn’t worth repairing anymore.
He found going to the Edinburgh Castle was worth it.
I’ll have a dollar's worth of candy, please.
They have proven their worths as individual fighting men and their worth as a unit.
Our new director is a man whose worth is well acknowledged.

definitions.text: An amount that could be achieved or produced in a specified time.

examples: Woe worth the man that crosses me.
